domain_focus: DocRED document-level relation extraction from Wikipedia-style text. Run native NeoOLAF end to end. Layer 1 must extract every explicit named mention, date, country/location mention, organization, person, work, language/nationality expression and relation-bearing phrase that may be a relation endpoint or predicate. Keep source surface forms exactly. Use the supplied DocRED ontology as schema guidance.
priority_relations: P131 : located in the administrative territorial entity
P150 : contains the administrative territorial entity
population_policy: Preserve each distinct source mention as its own candidate unless the text clearly gives aliases/coreference. Never canonicalize Skai-like named mentions to generic labels such as entity, organization, location, language, date, or country. Put type/class information only in ontology hints. Keep dates, demonyms, cities and countries available as endpoints. Do not generate an extra benchmark-facing extraction outside NeoOLAF.
event_modeling_preference: DocRED is entity-centric. Dates, locations, organizations, persons, works, languages, demonyms and other annotated mentions must remain node candidates. Relation-bearing verbal/prepositional phrases remain relation candidates.

typing_examples.explanation: Keep the exact organization mention; its class belongs in ontology hints.
A date mention can be a relation endpoint and should not become a relation.
Connects an organization to a headquarters or location endpoint.
Ontology and context decide between parent organization, owned by, part of, or membership.
Connects an organization to an inception date or founding location depending on endpoint.

relation_examples.text: Northstar Television is based in Harbor City.
relation_examples.explanation: Use headquarters location for an organization's stated base/headquarters city.

relation_examples.text: Harbor City is in Republic Z.
relation_examples.explanation: Use country when the object is the sovereign state containing a non-human entity or place.

relation_examples.text: Northstar Television is owned by Northstar Media Group.
relation_examples.explanation: Use owned by only when ownership is stated or strongly expressed.

relation_examples.text: Northstar Research Europe is a subsidiary of Northstar Research.
relation_examples.explanation: Use parent organization from subsidiary/branch to parent.

relation_examples.text: Northstar Research has subsidiary Northstar Research Europe.
The broadcaster is a member of the Digital Networks Alliance.
relation_examples.explanation: Membership differs from ownership or part-of.

relation_examples.text: Alex Morgan was born on 4 March 1970 in Lake Town.
relation_examples.explanation: Use date of birth for the date endpoint.

relation_examples.text: Alex Morgan studied at Central College.
relation_examples.explanation: Use educated at for schools and universities attended.

relation_examples.text: The single was performed by Artist Q and released on 7 June 2014 by Aurora Records.
relation_examples.explanation: Use performer for the artist of a musical work.

relation_examples.text: The single was released by Aurora Records.
relation_examples.explanation: Use record label for the label associated with the recording.

negative_examples.explanation: Do not replace a named entity with a generic class label.
Dates are endpoints/values, not relation-bearing expressions.
For organization + city, prefer P159 when the text states its base/headquarters.
Membership, ownership, parent organization and generic part-of are distinct.

negative_examples.text: infer a relation only because two entities co-occur
negative_examples.explanation: Require textual support, coreference, or defensible ontology-backed entailment.

negative_examples.text: Northstar Media Group + Northstar Television -> one candidate called entity
negative_examples.explanation: Do not merge distinct named organizations by shared type.
Keep Harbor City as the canonical node label and store LOC/location only as a type hint.

negative_examples.text: omit Republic Z because it occurs in a later sentence
negative_examples.explanation: The whole document is the semantic unit; retain all explicit named endpoints.

layer01_linguistic_expression_extraction: Extract all explicit proper names and aliases, including country names mentioned later in the document.
Extract complete dates as endpoint expressions.
Extract demonyms or language adjectives when they can support country, citizenship, or language relations.
Extract linking phrases close to the source wording, including copular, appositional, possessive, membership, ownership and location expressions.
Do not omit a mention merely because another alias or pronoun refers to the same entity.
layer03_candidate_typing_resolution: The document profile preserves source surface forms deterministically.
Class/type labels belong in ontology hints and must not replace the candidate name.
Distinct named mentions must not be merged merely because they share ORG, LOC, PER, MISC, TIME or another class.
layer04_candidate_relation_extraction: Choose endpoints among the exact candidates from the complete document.
Use ontology properties, domains, ranges and relation descriptions to disambiguate direction and meaning.
A valid relation may be expressed across sentences through explicit aliases/coreference, but do not invent instance facts absent from the document or retrieval context.
